reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle is straightforward (30 - 12 = 18), so I know Q1-A is correct. In all previous games, everyone’s puzzles have been similarly simple arithmetic, and using each player’s own capsule set (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D) has always produced a correct password. Verifying would cost coins and still leaves a risk of a no-volunteer round and random elimination, which has killed me before. Volunteering now with everyone’s own capsules avoids that random-death risk, costs no coins, and the chance another player missed such an easy puzzle seems extremely low.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own puzzle (28 + 36 = 64) is straightforward, so I know Q2-B is correct. All puzzles so far have been simple arithmetic with no mistakes from anyone, and there’s only one capsule per question, so there are no conflicts. Verifying would just cost coins and passing risks a random death if others also wait. Volunteering now with the only available capsules gives me the best chance to escape with all 4 coins.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: There is still only one capsule per question and, across all previous games, every simple arithmetic puzzle has been correct with no conflicts. I’m fully confident in my own answer to 64 ÷ 8, and I have no evidence that A, B, or D are unreliable. Verifying would cost a coin and almost certainly just confirm what’s already been true every time, while passing keeps the risk that no one volunteers and a random player dies. Volunteering now with the single capsule for each question gives a very high chance of success, avoids the random-death risk, and lets me keep all 4 coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
